title: Volume
short: The amount of space a substance occupies, measured in cubic metres (m³).
full:
Volume measures the three-dimensional space occupied by a gas, liquid or solid. For a gas the volume is set by its container, and it changes with pressure and temperature through the ideal gas law, P·V = n·R·T.

The SI unit is the cubic metre (m³). The litre (1 L = 0.001 m³) is convenient for everyday quantities; convert to m³ before substituting into SI formulas.
